If needed, parse or normalize academic_year to ensure proper chronological ordering.","Metric expression alias 'enrollment_count' and dimension aliases 'academic_year' and 'department_name' are the exact output field names the visualization expects."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"SELECT
  cs.academic_year AS academic_year,
  d.name AS department_name,
  SUM(CASE WHEN e.is_active = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS enrollment_count
FROM public.enrollments e
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
WHERE cs.academic_year IS NOT NULL
  AND e.is_active = true
GROUP BY cs.academic_year, d.name
ORDER BY cs.academic_year ASC, d.name ASC;","validationIssues":["academic_year is encoded as type 'ordinal' and the plan says academic_year is stored as text — if academic_year values are not lexicographically sortable you may get incorrect chronological ordering. data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach
Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach
Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

visualization.title: Enrollment trends by department over academic years

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT
  cs.academic_year AS academic_year,
  d.name AS department_name,
  SUM(CASE WHEN e.is_active = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS enrollment_count
FROM public.enrollments e
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
WHERE cs.academic_year IS NOT NULL
  AND e.is_active = true
GROUP BY cs.academic_year, d.name
ORDER BY cs.academic_year ASC, d.name ASC;

issues: academic_year is encoded as type 'ordinal' and the plan says academic_year is stored as text — if academic_year values are not lexicographically sortable you may get incorrect chronological ordering. Consider parsing/normalizing to a temporal type or providing an explicit sort order for years.
The SQL currently counts active enrollment rows (SUM(CASE WHEN e.is_active THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)). If you intended unique students per department-year, use COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) instead.

The query uses inner joins, so departments with zero enrollments will be excluded. If you want to show departments with zero enrollments, use a LEFT JOIN from departments and COALESCE the metric to 0.
If there are many departments, a single multi-line chart can become cluttered — consider filtering, interactive legend, or small multiples for clarity.

SELECT
  cs.academic_year AS academic_year,
  d.name AS department_name,
  SUM(CASE WHEN e.is_active = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS enrollment_count
FROM public.enrollments e
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
WHERE cs.academic_year IS NOT NULL
  AND e.is_active = true
GROUP BY cs.academic_year, d.name
ORDER BY cs.academic_year ASC, d.name ASC;

issues: academic_year is encoded as type 'ordinal' and the plan says academic_year is stored as text — if academic_year values are not lexicographically sortable you may get incorrect chronological ordering. Consider parsing/normalizing to a temporal type or providing an explicit sort order for years.
The SQL currently counts active enrollment rows (SUM(CASE WHEN e.is_active THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)). If you intended unique students per department-year, use COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) instead.

The query uses inner joins, so departments with zero enrollments will be excluded. If you want to show departments with zero enrollments, use a LEFT JOIN from departments and COALESCE the metric to 0.
If there are many departments, a single multi-line chart can become cluttered — consider filtering, interactive legend, or small multiples for clarity.
